trade.summary: Trade surged dramatically after 2022 as India became a primary buyer of discounted Russian crude oil. India now imports over 40% of its crude from Russia. India also imports fertilizers, coal, arms, and nuclear technology from Russia. India exports pharmaceuticals, engineering goods, electronics, and agricultural products to Russia.
disputes: Payment settlement issues due to Western sanctions on Russian banks

diplomatic.summary: India and Russia have a 'Special and Privileged Strategic Partnership', among the highest designations. Historical ties go back to the Soviet era. India has maintained its relationship with Russia despite the Ukraine war, abstaining at UN and refusing to sanction Russia. PM Modi visited Moscow in July 2024, the first such visit since the Ukraine invasion. India balances Russia ties carefully against growing US-West alignment.
recent_incidents: India abstained on UN resolutions on Ukraine (2022-2025)
PM Modi's Moscow visit criticized by Ukraine and Western partners (2024)

military.summary: Russia remains India's largest arms supplier historically, though India is diversifying. Major Russian systems in Indian service: Su-30MKI, MiG-29, T-90 tanks, S-400 air defence, INS Vikramaditya carrier, nuclear submarines under lease. S-400 acquisition triggered US CAATSA sanctions threat. India-Russia joint R&D continues under IRIGC-M&TC framework.

financial.summary: Russian bank sanctions have complicated financial flows. India is working on rupee-ruble payment mechanisms. Russian FDI in India is limited outside energy. Indian companies have stepped back from Russian market investments since 2022. Russian capital flows to India through third countries.

energy.summary: Russia has become India's top crude oil supplier (above Saudi Arabia) since 2022. India imports Russian coal, LNG (from Sakhalin), and is a co-investor in Russian LNG projects (Sakhalin-1). Russia is building nuclear power plants in India (Kudankulam). This energy dependence is a strategic lever for both sides.

scientific.summary: Historical scientific cooperation in space (ISRO-Roscosmos) and nuclear energy. Joint research continues in defence R&D, nuclear physics, and materials science. Russian universities host Indian students. Collaboration has been somewhat constrained post-2022 as Russian institutions face isolation from Western networks.
